results.name: James Faletagoai
results.ai_reason: Email contains standalone 4-digit number '61' which does not plausibly represent a birth year (1961 would make candidate 63 years old, over the 50-year threshold for labouring roles). However, CV explicitly states 'I am a 36 years old' which directly contradicts the age inference from email. The explicit age statement on the CV (36 years old) takes priority as the most reliable indicator per the rules and confirms candidate is under 50. However, the employment dates contain a critical issue: the most recent role shows 'January 2026 - March 2026' which is a future date (CV appears to be submitted before January 2026, or contains an error). This creates uncertainty about current employment status. Applying the 6-month employment requirement: if this is being reviewed in 2026 after March, the candidate would have no job in the last 6 months. The ambiguity in employment date clarity (unclear if January 2026 is genuine or a typo) combined with inability to confirm last 6 months of employment triggers rejection.
